reviews.review_description: One game on this I love, but I can't play with no one but the computer and I win every time. You start with your piece and you try and beat the other players to home base. You land on then and send them back to start. You roll 6 you can go again. I want to see more of these games but with private modes so I can play with my friends and family. Please. All the other games I'm not interested in. Sorry.

I bought a chess game from the same devs last year, which I enjoyed, so I thought I'd take a chance with this one despite there being no reviews. I was not disappointed. There is a great use of optional passthrough with the games. Loved the animated Ludo in passthrough. You can enlarge and shrink the boards to the size you want. The games runs very smoothly. I've been looking for board games like this for ages. 

P.S. - I had forgotten how long it takes to play a full game of Ludo, so start with a fully charged headset. :)

developer_response.body:
Hi dark-illusion46,

We appreciate your feedback on and are glad to hear that you enjoyed playing our game (both Chess and League of Tabletop Games haha). Our development team is constantly working to improve the gaming experience for all players, and your kind words serve as an inspiration to continue creating games that our players love. We are thrilled to see that our game resonated with you and hope that it provided you with entertainment and enjoyment.

Thank you for your support and for being a part of our community. We look forward to bringing you more great games in the future.

Best regards,
Suplife Team

reviews.review_description: Can we have some rules please!?! Or maybe I can't find it yet. Otherwise great job!

developer_response.body:
Hi Gooch 1504,

Thank you for leaving your feedback on League of Tabletop Games. Your constructive comments help us identify areas where we can improve the game and provide a better gaming experience for all players. We appreciate your valuable feedback and will take it into consideration as we continue to develop our games.

Meanwhile, here are the rules that may help you:
Ludo: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ludo
Chess: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rules_of_chess
Doudizhu: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dou_dizhu#Rules
ChineseChess: https://www.bristol.ac.uk/Depts/Union/CCC/chess-rules.htm

Kind Regards,
Suplife Team
